name: E03S02 — Magic Link Token Generator

nodes.name: Trigger: Job Assigned Webhook
nodes.notes:
Called by E02S02 (SMS assignment) after jobs.status is set to Assigned.
Expects POST body: { tech_id: '<uuid>' }
For E02S02 integration: replace this webhook trigger with the downstream node
sequence appended directly to the E02S02 workflow after the job PATCH step.

nodes.name: Generate Magic Link Token
PATCH techs — Store Token
nodes.notes:
AC2: PATCHes techs.magic_link_token and techs.token_issued_at.
AC3: ?id=eq.[tech_id] always overwrites the existing token row — no duplicates.
continueOnFail: true — PATCH failure must not halt the workflow or roll back the job assignment (AC5).

IMPORTANT: Replace 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY_OR_ANON_KEY' with your actual key.
Use the service_role key here (not anon) so the PATCH bypasses RLS. Alternatively,
ensure the anon role has UPDATE access on techs table (verify in Supabase → Auth → Policies).

REFERENCE PATTERN (n8n 2.x): $('Generate Magic Link Token').item.json.tech_id
NOT $json.tech_id — upstream node data must be referenced by node name.

nodes.name: Token PATCH Succeeded?
nodes.notes:
AC5: Checks whether the PATCH returned a valid row (return=representation).
True branch (PATCH succeeded) → workflow ends cleanly.
False branch (PATCH failed or returned no row) → Log Token Error node.
Either way: the upstream job assignment is NEVER rolled back.

nodes.name: Log Token Error
nodes.notes:
AC5: Logs the PATCH failure for debugging. Workflow ends here.
The job assignment (jobs table) is NOT reverted — assignment stands.
This node executes only on the false branch of 'Token PATCH Succeeded?'

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes:
Webhook response — sends 200 OK back to the caller.
For E02S02 integration (no webhook trigger): remove this node; the flow ends at 'Token PATCH Succeeded?'

_comment:
INTEGRATION NOTES — READ BEFORE IMPORTING

This workflow has TWO integration modes:

MODE 1: Standalone webhook (current setup)
  - Import as-is into n8n
  - Webhook path: POST https://nexusfound.cloud/webhook/sendjob-magic-link
  - Body: { 'tech_id': '<uuid>' }
  - E02S02 (SMS assignment) must call this webhook after setting jobs.status=Assigned
  - E02S07 (dashboard) handles token generation client-side (see dispatcher-dashboard/index.html)

MODE 2: Inline node sequence (recommended for E02S02)
  - Copy nodes 'Generate Magic Link Token', 'PATCH techs — Store Token',
    'Token PATCH Succeeded?', and 'Log Token Error' into the E02S02 workflow
  - Connect 'PATCH jobs (Assign)' → 'Generate Magic Link Token'
  - Remove the 'Trigger: Job Assigned Webhook' and 'Respond to Webhook' nodes
  - The tech_id reference in Generate Magic Link Token changes to:
    $('PATCH jobs (Assign)').item.json.tech_id
    (use the actual node name from E02S02 workflow that writes tech_id to jobs)

SUPABASE KEY:
  - Replace 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY_OR_ANON_KEY' in the PATCH node with the real key
  - Preferred: service_role key (bypasses RLS) — store in n8n Credentials as env variable
  - Alternative: anon key — requires UPDATE policy on techs table for anon role
    (Supabase → Auth → Policies → techs table → check anon UPDATE policy)

FIELD REFERENCES (n8n 2.x pattern — critical):
  - ALWAYS: $('Node Name').item.json.fieldname
  - NEVER: $json.fieldname for upstream node data
  - Code node output: return [{ json: { ... } }]
  - Expression mode: toggle orange '=' icon per field
